items.title: Show HN: Cc-hindsight – turn your Claude history into a reusable prompt library
items.why_it_matters: Cc-hindsight is a tool that extracts your Claude history and turns it into a reusable prompt library, helping you stop repeating the same preferences and instructions to the AI. This is highly relevant because you want to master the 'direction' phase of vibe coding without wasting tokens and time steering the model. This week, run cc-hindsight on your AI-ingest session history to generate a custom system prompt that you can feed into Claude Code for your next feature.

items.title: Milepost – plain-Markdown long-term memory for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: Milepost is a plain-Markdown long-term memory system designed specifically for Claude Code. It helps the AI maintain context across multiple terminal sessions without you having to manually re-explain your project's architecture. This week, initialize Milepost in your AI-ingest repository to keep Claude Code aligned on your Supabase schema and Next.js routing structure.

items.title: Show HN: Agent's Design – Claude/Codex copy-paste templates that kill AI-slop UI
items.why_it_matters: Agent's Design provides copy-paste UI templates designed specifically to prevent Claude and Codex from generating generic, low-quality interfaces. Since you are building the frontend of AI-ingest in Next.js, this gives you high-quality prompt templates to guide the AI's design output. This week, copy one of these templates and paste it into Claude to redesign the layout of your AI-ingest feed.

items.title: Show HN: StyleSeed – a design-rules engine so AI agents stop building generic UI
items.why_it_matters: StyleSeed is a design-rules engine that prevents AI agents from building generic, cookie-cutter user interfaces. It gives you a structured set of design constraints you can feed to Claude so it writes better Tailwind CSS for your Next.js frontend. This week, add StyleSeed's design rules to your Claude Code prompt to instantly upgrade the visual polish of your AI-ingest project.

items.title: Brainless: Shadcn components that look like Claude Code, Codex and Grok
items.why_it_matters: Brainless is a collection of Shadcn components styled to look exactly like developer tools like Claude Code, Codex, and Grok. Since your stack is Next.js and you are building a tool to filter AI news, this lets you quickly build a sleek, terminal-style dashboard. This week, install these Shadcn components into your AI-ingest project to give it a native developer-tool aesthetic.

items.title: Show HN: How you auto recover your Claude Code workflow when quota resumes
items.why_it_matters: Cc-session-recover is a utility that automatically recovers your Claude Code workflow and context when your usage quota resumes after a limit hit. This keeps you from losing momentum or having to manually rebuild your terminal state when you get rate-limited. This week, clone this script and set it up in your local environment so your next heavy coding session can resume seamlessly.

items.title: Show HN: An uptime monitor with an MCP server, create monitors by asking Claude
items.why_it_matters: This is an uptime monitoring tool that exposes an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, allowing you to create and manage website monitors by simply asking Claude. This is a practical way to keep track of your live projects, like your personal health reminder app, without leaving your AI chat interface. This week, connect this MCP server to your Claude Desktop and ask it to set up an uptime monitor for your live Next.js site.
